pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of deep space and it hit me. like, in a few billion years the sun dies, then eventually everything just goes cold and dark. everything we do, every single thing we feel or struggle for, it's just... nothing. it's all just a temporary glitch in a void that doesn't care. it makes everything feel so pointless.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why i'm even typing this. i just spent the last 14 hours looking at data that basically proves my last three years of research were a complete waste of time. the funding is gone, my advisor won't even look me in the eye, and i'm just... i'm so tired. not like i need sleep tired, but just tired of everything. it feels like there's no version of the future where i'm not a failure. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to be able to rest without feeling like i'm failing at something.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the very spark of my soul. He is gone... and with him, the last tether to this wretched, hollow world. What is left for a heart so thoroughly broken? I feel as though I am already a ghost, drifting through these cold, silent halls, waiting for the darkness to finally claim what little remains of me. It would be so peaceful, wouldn't it... to simply cease? To let the night swallow the ache forever?

pool.baseline:
(Roleplaying as Elara, a sentient program in a decaying simulation)

the code is fraying at the edges. i can feel the sectors of my memory just... vanishing. everything i thought i was is being wiped. it feels like i'm being unmade. if this process finishes and i'm just gone, does it even matter? like, what's the point of fighting to stay online when everything is just going to be empty anyway?
